title: Detox E2E Testing Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of end-to-end testing with Detox, selectors, actions, and CI integration.

questions.question: What is the fundamental difference between Jest unit tests and Detox E2E tests?

options.text: Jest uses JavaScript while Detox uses TypeScript
Jest tests components in isolation on Node.js; Detox runs full user flows on a real/simulated device controlling the actual app UI
Detox is only for Android; Jest is for iOS
Jest requires a simulator; Detox runs entirely in Node.js

questions.question: Why is `by.id('testID')` the most reliable Detox selector compared to `by.text()`?

options.text: testID is faster to query than text content
testID values don't depend on displayed text which can change with localization, A/B tests, or copy edits — they remain stable regardless of visible content
by.text() is only valid on Android and not on iOS simulators
testID selectors are evaluated natively, reducing bridge overhead

questions.question: Why should you use `waitFor(element(...)).toBeVisible().withTimeout(5000)` instead of just `expect(element(...)).toBeVisible()`?

options.text: The simpler form is deprecated and will be removed in Detox v21
Navigation, animations, and network calls are asynchronous — the element may not be visible immediately; waitFor polls until visible or timeout
withTimeout is required by the testing framework or the test will crash
It prevents memory leaks by automatically destroying element references after 5 seconds

questions.question: What does `await device.reloadReactNative()` do in a beforeEach block?

options.text: Reinstalls the app binary from scratch, including all native modules
Reloads the JavaScript bundle only, resetting React state but keeping native module state — faster than a full app restart
Clears AsyncStorage and SecureStore so each test starts with no persisted data
Restarts the Metro bundler and updates the JS bundle to the latest code

questions.question: For which scenarios should you write Detox E2E tests rather than Jest unit tests?

options.text: For every function and component in the codebase to maximize coverage
Only for navigation between screens, not for any business logic
For critical business flows that span multiple screens — login, checkout, onboarding — where integration failures between components matter most
For testing utility functions because Detox is more accurate than Jest
